note: Asian refined products are critical global signals. Singapore Gasoil is the Asian diesel benchmark. Fuel Oil 380CST tracks shipping demand. Naphtha drives petrochemical activity.

sections.title: Asian Diesel
sections.subtitle: Singapore Gasoil — global diesel benchmark for Asia

products.name: Singapore Gasoil 10ppm
products.context: The benchmark for Asian diesel pricing. Tracks demand from manufacturing, trucking, and shipping across Asia. Spread vs European diesel (ICE Gasoil) shows global flow imbalances — when Asia trades premium, European refiners ship east.

products.name: Singapore Naphtha
products.context: Singapore is Asia's naphtha trading hub. Higher prices = stronger petrochemical demand from China/Korea ethylene crackers. Cracker margins (naphtha vs ethylene) drive plant utilization decisions.

products.name: Japan Naphtha CFR
products.context: Japan is the world's largest naphtha importer. CFR cargoes show what Japanese petchem buyers are paying delivered. Note unit is $/MT (metric ton), not $/bbl. Premium vs Singapore reflects shipping + insurance to Japan.

products.name: South Korea Naphtha
products.context: South Korea hosts massive ethylene crackers (LG Chem, Lotte). Korean naphtha demand is a leading indicator for global plastics manufacturing. High prices = strong consumer goods demand or cracker margin squeeze.

sections.title: Bunker Fuel · Shipping Signal
sections.subtitle: Marine fuel — proxy for global shipping volumes

products.name: Singapore Fuel Oil 380CST
products.context: The world's busiest bunker fueling port. Singapore 380CST prices track demand from container ships, tankers, and bulk carriers globally. When BDI (Baltic Dry Index) rises, bunker demand rises with it. Useful cross-check for shipping ETF investors.

products.name: Singapore Fuel Oil 180CST
products.context: Lower-viscosity bunker grade, premium to 380CST. Used in older or smaller vessels. Spread vs 380CST narrows when refining capacity is tight.

products.name: Singapore Gasoline 92 RON
products.context: Asia's gasoline benchmark grade. Tracks driving demand across India, Indonesia, Vietnam, and other emerging Asian markets. Asian gasoline prices often diverge from US RBOB — Asia is a net importer where the US is a net exporter.
